steps.title: Find the central conflict
steps.content:
The passage presents a crucial contrast: 'I had to act really happy because it was such a big present, but I pretty much hated the thing from the second it was carried into the hallway upstairs.' This tension between forced behavior and true feeling is reinforced by the emotional language that follows: 'The piano glared at me.

It was like a songbird in a cage.' The passage is anchored in revealing authentic emotion.

steps.title: Match the purpose
steps.content:
Choice B directly matches this purpose: it states the passage establishes how the narrator feels about the piano. The other choices miss the real function—
  • A wrongly suggests she wanted the piano (she hated it)
  • D focuses on how the piano was acquired rather than the narrator's emotional stance
  • C addresses the brothers' talents
, which the passage never discusses.

That matches choice B — B is correct.
